title: Senior Business Analyst Defense (w/m/d)
required_skills: Several years experience as Business Analyst (or Product Owner/Business Solution Designer) in IT/digital programmes
Defense background (industry, public sector, or military/Offizierslaufbahn) with existing Bundeswehr/defense network
Domain expertise in at least one defense area (e.g. Führungsunterstützung, logistics/maintenance, ISR, PLM/ALM, cyber security processes)
Business Analysis methods: requirements engineering, process/data modelling, functional architecture, business case
Stakeholder management and change enablement: moderation, conflict resolution, storytelling, transformation support
Willingness for security clearance (SÜG) and travel
matched_skills: Stakeholder management and moderation experience (HSBC, Hapag-Lloyd)
Agile sprint experience: Scrum ceremonies, backlog support (Hapag-Lloyd) — partial bonus hit
missing_skills: Zero defense industry or Bundeswehr background
No security clearance (SÜG) — would require vetting process
No business analysis formal methodology (BPMN, UML, requirements engineering)
No domain expertise in any defense area (logistics, ISR, cyber, PLM/ALM)
No consulting background or client advisory experience
German B1 — role requires C1+/business fluency in German
core_responsibilities: End-to-end business analysis in defense programmes (Bundeswehr, agencies, defense industry)
Translate AI/data use cases into functional architectures and solution scenarios
Stakeholder management and change enablement in AI-adoption contexts
Build and maintain defense sector network
recommended_emphasis: Do not apply — defense domain expertise is the primary selection criterion and the candidate has none
Security clearance requirement adds additional barrier
German B1 is insufficient for this German-language-heavy consulting role
Even the business analysis methodology is largely missing
summary: Match score is 11.1 (poor_match). This is a specialist defense consulting role with hard requirements around Bundeswehr domain knowledge, security clearance, and German business fluency. The candidate has none of these. The only transferable skills are general stakeholder management and Jira experience, which are insufficient to compensate for the profound domain and language gaps. Do not apply.
